channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks parts of Trump's executive order seeking to punish law firm Perkins Coie
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge blocked President Donald Trump's administration Wednesday from enforcing portions of an executive order designed to punish a prominent law firm linked to Democratic-funded opposition research during the 2016 presidential campaign into ties between the Republican can
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to photograph the total lunar eclipse
item.description: You could see the full moon turn blood red (or burnt red) early Friday morning as the earth transits between the moon and the sun. And if luck is on your side, there will not be any clouds covering this celestial sight.
item.dc:creator: Duncan Phenix, KRON4

item.title: New Haven police reconnect father with daughter who was kidnapped in 1999
item.description: New Haven police have reconnected a father and his daughter who was kidnapped decades ago from New Haven.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Passenger attacked flight attendant, swallowed rosary beads on plane to Miami: court docs
item.description: His sister told the FBI agent that they were traveling to Haiti “to flee religious attacks of a spiritual nature.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Migrant gang member suspected of murder, sexually assaulting girlfriend is arrested
item.description: A Venezuelan illegal migrant and suspected Tren de Aragua gang member was arrested in Chicago for a fourth time Tuesday after first being ordered deported under the Biden administration in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman catches man driving her stolen car
item.description: A man is now in custody and facing an auto theft charge after a woman allegedly caught him driving her stolen car.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: 5 men accused of blocking traffic with twerking
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (WREG) -- Five people were arrested Monday night after police say they blocked a downtown intersection with their dancing.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police reconnect father and daughter who was kidnapped decades ago
item.description: Police are announcing they've re-connected a father and his daughter, who was kidnapped from New Haven decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: American student, 20, vanishes in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: Authorities are continuing efforts to find Sudiksha Konanki, a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student who has been missing for nearly a week while on a spring break trip in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump is dismantling the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has said he wants his new education chief, Linda McMahon, to “put herself out of a job.” Since Trump took office, staffing at the Education Department has been cut in half, and he has been mulling an executive order to close the agency.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Study shows rain-soaking atmospheric rivers are getting bigger, wetter and more frequent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As extreme weather events have hit the world hard in recent years, one meteorology term — atmospheric rivers — has made the leap from scientific circles to common language, particularly in places that have been hit by them. That stands to reason.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NOAA set to fire 1,029 more employees
item.description: Staffing levels at the nation's already hobbled weather, climate and oceans agency could take a further nosedive in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans warn Trump not to let trade war drag as he doubles down on tariff threats
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning President Trump to not let his trade war drag on as his latest escalation sent Wall Street further into turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote was not the decisive one, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KRON4

item.title: White House pressures Columbia University as it seeks to deport pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House complained Tuesday that Columbia University is refusing to help federal agents find people being sought as part of the government’s effort to deport participants in pro-Palestinian demonstrations, as the administration continued to punish the school by yanking federal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Time is running out for thousands to claim $1 billion in 2021 tax refunds
item.description: Over 1.1 million people have unclaimed federal income tax refunds from 2021, which are in jeopardy of becoming government property if not claimed soon, with a median return of $781 and potential refunds worth up to $6,728 in Earned Income Tax Credit.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: TROY, Mich. (AP) — Four people have been charged in the death of a 5-year-old boy who was “incinerated” inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at a suburban Detroit medical facility, Michigan’s attorney general said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US bans flights to Haiti's capital until Sept. 8 as UN expert says gang violence is more dire
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Tuesday extended its ban on flights to Haiti’s capital until Sept. 8 because of escalating gang violence, which the U.N.’s human rights expert on the Caribbean nation said is more dire than ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NTSB urges ban on some helicopter flights at Reagan airport after deadly collision
item.description: Federal investigators looking for the cause of the January collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people recommended a ban on some helicopter flights Tuesday to improve safety.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How microplastics may be stoking antibiotic resistance
item.description: The microscopic shards of plastic found in every corner of the planet may be exacerbating antibiotic resistance, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, KRON4

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Infowars employee killed in Texas, law enforcement confirms
item.description: Jamie White, an Infowars employee, was the victim of a homicide in southeast Austin, Texas, late Sunday night, law enforcement sources confirmed to Nexstar's KXAN.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, ABC 4

item.title: Why you'll no longer see 'excessive heat warnings' in the forecast
item.description: It may be hard to think about sweltering temperatures in early March but excessive heat watches and warnings are getting a new name.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works; Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Father of missing US student Sudiksha Konanki asks for wider investigation in Dominican Republic
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The father of a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki, who went missing in the popular Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana while on spring break with friends, said he has asked authorities to widen their investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stocks plunge as Wall Street wonders how much economic pain Trump will accept
item.description: The economy has already given some signals of weakening, mostly through surveys showing increased pessimism.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stroller accessory recalled after 6 children suffer finger lacerations: CPSC
item.description: Consumers who may have purchased or installed the accessories are being instructed to immediately discontinue use.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges against Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy
item.description: Xavier Worthy was arrested Friday and has since been released after the county's district attorney said he would not accept the case at this time.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Do you need fluoride after a teeth cleaning? Dental experts weigh in
item.description: Dentists and hygienists often propose fluoride treatments to patients in the dental chair to prevent cavities and strengthen teeth. But the relatively simple procedure – which involves applying a varnish, gel or foam directly to the teeth – isn’t always covered by insurance, especially for adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history
item.description: Garrett, the 2023 AP Defensive Player of the Year, had asked for a trade last month but the Browns were adamant about keeping the 29-year-old star in Cleveland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Public, private, and political: Valentino turns a staged restroom into high fashion’s boldest stage
item.description: Valentino paraded high fashion inside public toilets in one of the season’s most provocative backdrops, especially for a house as classical as Valentino — upending traditional notions of luxury.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio couple charged after hundreds of living and dead rats found inside home
item.description: A Stark County couple is facing animal cruelty charges after authorities discovered horrific conditions inside their home, which included hundreds of living and dead rats.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, Fox 5
